instructions: These rules define HOW to classify each case type. ALWAYS cross-reference email text with document evidence. Never classify purely on email wording — brokers often mislabel submissions.

new_claim.description: A risk that IAT has NOT previously insured. First-time submission for this insured with IAT specifically.

confirms: broker_cover_letter introducing the risk for the first time
blank or empty application/claim forms (no pre-filled IAT policy data)
no policy schedule from IAT is present
contradicts: An existing IAT policy schedule is attached
Any document contains an IAT policy number
Renewal notice or expiry reminder from IAT

misleading_phrases_to_ignore: renewal — note: broker may call it renewal because the insured is renewing elsewhere, but it is still new_claim for IAT if no IAT policy exists

new_claim.override_rule: CRITICAL: Even if the email uses the word 'renewal', classify as new_claim if NO existing IAT policy number can be found anywhere in the email or attached documents. A risk renewing with another carrier but being submitted to IAT for the first time is ALWAYS new_claim. The IAT policy number pattern is: IAT- or IAT/ prefix.
new_claim.confidence_boost: If no IAT policy number found anywhere AND documents are blank application forms → confidence should be 0.90+

renewal.description: Re-insuring an EXISTING IAT policy at or before its expiry date.

confirms: Current IAT policy schedule attached (shows IAT policy number, inception/expiry dates)
Renewal invitation or expiry notice from IAT
Pre-filled application referencing an existing IAT policy number
contradicts: No IAT policy schedule or policy number found anywhere
Documents are blank application forms with no existing policy reference
Broker introduction letter that introduces the risk as new

email_signals.required_evidence: Email alone is NOT sufficient. There must be at least one of: (a) an IAT policy number referenced, (b) an IAT policy schedule document attached, (c) expiry/inception dates matching an existing IAT policy.

renewal.override_rule: CRITICAL: Do NOT classify as renewal if the only evidence is the broker saying 'renewal' in the email but no IAT policy number or current IAT policy schedule can be found. Downgrade to new_claim in that case.
renewal.confidence_boost: If IAT policy number present in documents AND policy schedule attached → confidence 0.92+

endorsement.description: A change to an existing live IAT policy (coverage adjustment, name change, address change, etc.).

confirms: Letter requesting a specific change to existing policy

endorsement.required_evidence: Must reference an existing IAT policy number AND describe a specific change requested.
endorsement.override_rule: If the change described is major (more than 25% exposure increase, new line of business), consider new_claim instead.

bor.description: Transfer of broker authority for an existing IAT policy from one agency to another.

confirms: BOR letter or Broker of Record letter signed by insured

bor.override_rule: BOR ALWAYS takes priority over other categories if a BOR letter is present, even if other submission content exists.

general_query.description: A question or information request not tied to a specific active submission. No documents requiring processing.

confirms: No substantive attachments, or only correspondence/email chains attached

general_query.override_rule: If a substantive document (claim_form, policy_schedule, broker_cover_letter) is attached, this is likely NOT a general_query — re-evaluate.

follow_up.description: Chasing or updating a previously submitted case. The initial submission already exists.

confirms: Forwarded email chain showing original submission
Correspondence referencing a previous submission date or reference number

follow_up.override_rule: If this looks like a follow-up BUT attaches new substantive documents not seen before, consider new_claim or renewal instead.

complaint_escalation.description: A formal complaint, dispute, or escalation requiring management attention.
complaint_escalation.override_rule: complaint_escalation ALWAYS takes priority over general_query if complaint language is present. Never downgrade a complaint to general_query.

regulatory_legal.description: Communication from a regulator, legal firm, or compliance authority. Not a broker submission.

documentation_evidence.description: Supporting documents for an already-processed case. Not a new submission, just additional evidence or paperwork.
documentation_evidence.override_rule: Only use this if the documents are clearly supplementary to an existing case, not a new submission.

spam_irrelevant.description: Not an insurance submission. Marketing, personal email, or completely off-topic.
spam_irrelevant.override_rule: Be conservative — only classify as spam if you are very sure (confidence > 0.90). When in doubt, use general_query.

decision_process: STEP 1 — Look for IAT policy number in ALL documents and email body. Pattern: starts with IAT- or IAT/. This single check resolves the new_claim vs renewal ambiguity.

STEP 2 — Check document types. The presence of a policy_schedule strongly confirms renewal. The presence of only claim_form + broker_cover_letter with no IAT policy number strongly confirms new_claim.
STEP 3 — Apply override rules. BOR overrides everything. complaint_escalation overrides general_query.
STEP 4 — Only then use email body language as a tiebreaker, not the primary signal.
STEP 5 — If still unsure, prefer new_claim over renewal, and general_query over spam_irrelevant.
